title: Regularized OOF target encoding stack for high-cardinality IDs
summary: Benchmarks on high-cardinality categoricals found regularized target encoding consistently strong.
rationale: Benchmarks on high-cardinality categoricals found regularized target encoding consistently strong. Here `Driver` has 800+ levels, and interaction IDs like `Driver x Race`, `Driver x Compound`, and `Race x LapBucket` are likely informative if encoded with strict out-of-fold shrinkage rather than naive frequency counts.
implementation_hint: Build leakage-safe encoders only from training folds: empirical-Bayes / GLMM / CatBoost-style encodings for `Driver`, `Race_Year`, `Driver_Compound`, `Driver_Race`, `Compound_Stint`, and coarse lap buckets. Compare adding them to LightGBM/XGBoost against current frequency features.
expected_effect: Stronger entity priors and interaction signal with less variance than one-hot or raw frequency features, especially for medium-frequency drivers and race contexts.
risk: Easy to overstate CV if the encoding is not fully fold-pure or time-aware within race sequences.
